project.name: life-finance
project.description: Life Finance is a browser-first financial-life simulation backed by an exact, replayable TypeScript engine, PostgreSQL persistence, a version-pinned PolicyEngine tax service, and bounded educational AI roles.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the root README to understand Life Finance as a browser-first financial-life simulation and to see its production services, API surface, setup, and deployment shape. It also establishes the central authority rule for the rest of the tour: deterministic TypeScript owns financial truth, while persistence, tax, and AI sit behind explicit boundaries.

tour.title: Website Entry Shell
tour.description: With the product purpose established, follow the primary website entry route into the shared Next.js layout. The root page delegates presentation to onboarding, while the layout supplies global metadata, styles, navigation, and the game-state provider that every route inherits.
tour.languageLesson: Next.js App Router layouts persist shared UI and providers across nested routes, while page modules define the content for one route segment.

tour.title: Play Console Flow
tour.description: From the website shell, move to the developer Play route and its top-level console, which exercises onboarding, run hydration, commands, time advancement, events, milestones, teaching, and debriefs end to end. The typed HTTP client is the console's bridge to the authoritative versioned APIs, keeping browser behavior aligned with server contracts.
tour.languageLesson: The React console acts as an orchestration component: it composes smaller panels and state transitions while delegating domain authority to server APIs.

tour.title: Authoritative V2 API
tour.description: The play client reaches thin Next.js route handlers such as bounded time advancement, then passes through shared parsing and error translation into the v2 application service. Versioned schemas define the wire contract, while the service coordinates creation, commands, previews, checkpoints, causal history, migration, and counterfactuals without moving financial rules into HTTP code.

tour.title: State Money Evidence
tour.description: Below the API service are the highest-fan-in domain foundations: branded money, safe simulation months, immutable v2 state, canonical hashing, and the double-entry ledger. Together they make exact arithmetic, chronology, snapshots, provenance, reversals, and replay identities explicit rather than incidental implementation details.
tour.languageLesson: TypeScript branded integer types distinguish cents, rates, and months at compile time; canonical serialization then gives structurally equal values stable hashes for replay and evidence.

tour.title: Monthly Simulation Engine
tour.description: Building on those primitives, the monthly-turn orchestrator coordinates markets, the financial close, obligations, insurance, events, risk, balance controls, and outcome grading. The financial kernel performs the authoritative close, the transition module accepts it into state with continuity checks, and the time controller repeats bounded months until a decision or terminal outcome requires a pause.

tour.title: Authority Design Guides
tour.description: Pause after the engine code to consolidate its architectural rules in the repository, state-and-ledger, and financial-engine guides. These documents explain dependency direction, authoritative versus derived values, exact rounding, sparse snapshots, replay, corruption handling, and the boundaries contributors must preserve when changing the system.

tour.title: Events Balance Outcomes
tour.description: Within each monthly turn, deterministic event eligibility and lifecycle handling introduce player decisions without surrendering replayability. Scenario direction and runtime balancing rank and filter candidates under policy constraints, while the outcomes module determines retirement or bankruptcy and records obligation funding through auditable ledger effects.

tour.title: Persistence and Replay
tour.description: After an in-memory transition is accepted, the Drizzle schema and transactional run repository persist authoritative state, commands, evidence, ledger rows, outbox work, and sparse replay checkpoints. The initial migration establishes the relational foundation, while the latest migration decouples accepted commands from mandatory snapshots so verified replay can remain sparse rather than duplicating every state.
tour.languageLesson: Ordered SQL migrations evolve a deployed schema incrementally; constraints and indexes preserve invariants that application-level validation alone cannot guarantee.

tour.title: Pinned Tax Boundary
tour.description: Tax estimates cross a separately deployed service boundary rather than entering the deterministic TypeScript engine as hidden policy logic. FastAPI authenticates requests, Pydantic models enforce household contracts, the calculator maps values into pinned 2026 PolicyEngine rules, and the service guide plus Vercel configuration document its accuracy and deployment limits.
tour.languageLesson: FastAPI decorators expose Python endpoint functions, while Pydantic models validate and serialize the request and response boundary before PolicyEngine is invoked.

tour.title: Bounded AI Teaching
tour.description: Unlike the tax service, AI is optional and deliberately non-authoritative: role contracts bound its output, privacy filters redact sensitive data, and game-context projection supplies stable evidence facts. The role-aware client audits constrained invocations, while the teaching service preserves deterministic checkpoints and presentation models even when rewrites or narrative providers are unavailable.

tour.title: Verification and Delivery
tour.description: Finish with the repository's executable quality gates and their recorded system-level evidence. The package manifest defines local verification commands, CI runs separate Node and Python jobs plus the deterministic balance gate, the final journey suite crosses domain, persistence, tax, AI, and teaching boundaries, and the integration audit records remaining limitations and authority conclusions.
tour.languageLesson: GitHub Actions workflows use jobs for independent verification environments and sequential steps within each job, making Node and Python quality gates explicit and repeatable.
